  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Worker tied off to a temporary guardrail top rail instead of an approved anchorage point.",
    "Guardrail is designed for a 200-pound force limit, which is insufficient for personal fall arrest system (PFAS) anchorage requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Order the worker to immediately step back from the roof edge and disconnect from the guardrail.",
    "Require the worker to use the engineered anchor point 40 feet away with an appropriate lifeline/lanyard setup, or install a temporary certified anchor closer to the work area.",
    "Conduct a safety stand-down to reinforce that convenience or short task duration does not justify bypassing fall protection standards."
  ],
  "rationale": "A standard guardrail is designed to withstand a 200-pound force, whereas a personal fall arrest anchorage point must support at least 5,000 pounds per employee attached (or be designed as part of a complete system with a safety factor of two). Using a guardrail as an anchor can cause structural failure of the guardrail during a fall, resulting in a catastrophic double-hazard (fall of the worker and collapse of the perimeter protection). Task duration does not exempt the work from fall protection regulations.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926.502(d)(15)",
    "OSHA 1926.502(b)(3)"
  ]
}
